# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL Client Authentication Configuration File
# ===================================================

# This file controls which hosts are allowed to connect to this
# PostgreSQL server, and which authentication method is used for
# those hosts. Refer to the PostgreSQL documentation for a
# complete description.

# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# Local connections:
# METHOD can be "scram-sha-256", "md5", "peer", "ident", "trust", "reject"

# "local" connections are Unix domain socket connections
local   all             all                                     peer

# IPv4 local connections:
# This entry allows connections from the local machine itself for all users
# using scram-sha-256 for password authentication.
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256

# Host-based authentication for application servers
# =================================================

# This section configures authentication rules for our production PostgreSQL deployment.
# We assume application servers are on a specific subnet, e.g., 10.0.1.0/24
# Replace '10.0.1.0/24' with your actual application server subnet or IP range.

# Admin users: Only specific admin users can connect from the admin workstation IP.
# Replace 'admin_user1,admin_user2' with your actual admin usernames.
# Replace '192.168.1.100/32' with your actual admin workstation IP.
host    all             admin           192.168.1.100/32        scram-sha-256

# Application users: Connections from application servers for the 'app' role.
# These users will connect to specific databases, so we specify 'prod_db_app'.
# Replace 'prod_db_app' with your actual application database name.
# Replace 'app' with your actual application username.
# Replace '10.0.1.0/24' with your actual application server subnet.
host    prod_db_app     app             10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Read-only users: Connections from specific analytics/reporting servers for the 'readonly' role.
# Replace 'prod_db_readonly' with the database name for read-only access.
# Replace 'readonly' with your actual read-only username.
# Replace '10.0.2.0/24' with your actual reporting server subnet.
host    prod_db_readonly readonly        10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Example for replication user (if needed)
# host    replication     replicator      10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Deny all other connections (optional, but good for security)
# This rule should be at the end, as rules are evaluated in order.
# host    all             all             all                     reject
